justifications.overall: The extraction is partially informative due to rich attribute fields, but numerous missing actors, incorrect track alignment, and omitted transport details limit its utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only lists 3 pedestrians and 2 vehicles while the ground truth contains 7 persons and 4 vehicles, so many objects are missing and coverage is poor.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only one pedestrian and two vehicles despite six persons and four vehicles in the ground truth, so it misses most objects.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only 3 pedestrians (vs 7 in ground truth) and 2 vehicles (vs 4), omitting several actors and many per-object attributes.

justifications.completeness: Many persons and all vehicles from the ground truth are missing or not matched, only three pedestrian entries were reported.

justifications.completeness: Extraction lists only four pedestrians versus six in ground truth and omits at least one moving vehicle, so many ground-truth agents and key attributes are missing.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 5 ground-truth persons and only 1 of 4 ground-truth vehicles, so overall instance coverage is limited.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures some key actors but omits at least one pedestrian (obj_track_id 5) and two vehicles from the ground truth, covering roughly half of the annotated objects.

justifications.completeness: Captures 3 of 5 pedestrians and 1 of 3 vehicles, missing two pedestrians and two vehicles.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only three pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth lists seven persons (including a bicyclist) and one vehicle, so coverage is low.

justifications.completeness: Detected only three pedestrians and four vehicle-like objects while ground truth contains ten persons and five vehicles, so many actors are missing.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies only one pedestrian and two vehicles while the ground truth contains multiple additional persons and vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian is reported versus seven in the ground truth and multiple actors are entirely missing, so coverage is very low.

justifications.completeness: Detected all six pedestrians but only one of six vehicles was reported, so overall coverage is partial.

justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are detailed and plausible in isolation but are often assigned to incorrect or spurious tracks and sometimes internally inconsistent, reducing their reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 pedestrians out of 7 annotated and omits multiple vehicles, so most ground-truth objects are missing.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth pedestrians (five of seven) are missing and vehicle track IDs are not matched, so overall coverage is poor.

justifications.completeness: Detected only three of five ground truth persons, missed the person carrying an item and misclassified a senior as adult, though the parked vehicle was identified.

justifications.completeness: Missing one of two pedestrians and one of three vehicles from the ground truth, so coverage is low.

justifications.spatial_relations: Includes useful relations (parked on right, pedestrian near curb) but contains ID inconsistencies and lacks relations for the missing objects.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures meaningful semantics and spatial cues but notable omissions and track ID mismatches reduce its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Misses one of two annotated pedestrians and omits one vehicle from the ground truth, and track ids do not match.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful attributes such as vehicle type, motion status and door/trunk states and several contexts, but omits the second person's action and some contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives detailed spatial relations for detected objects that partially match ground truth (pedestrian near curb) but introduces unverified relations and lacks relations for the missing person and other vehicle.
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially accurate with helpful semantic details but incomplete object coverage and some unverified spatial claims reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of two ground-truth persons and one vehicle but missed the second person and used nonmatching track ids.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes (actions, contexts, age, communicative, transporting, motion status, spatial relations) beyond the ground truth, though it adds assumed details like explicit trunk/door states and an unrelated track id.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are detailed and consistent with the scene description (near right curb, parked by left building, walking away) but relations for the missing person are absent and track ids do not match.
justifications.overall: Generally accurate and semantically rich for the detected objects but reduced reliability due to missed person, track id mismatches, and some assumed object attributes.

justifications.completeness: Both pedestrians from the ground truth are detected but the annotated parked vehicle is missing and some person context labels differ from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many attributes and detailed spatial descriptors beyond the ground truth, though some attributes such as transporting are incorrect or unsupported by the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for both pedestrians but the first person is localized inconsistently with the ground truth and the second person's relations add details not present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction is informative and captures the main actors but contains notable omissions and some incorrect attribute assignments, yielding a moderately accurate result.

justifications.completeness: The extraction fully describes the pedestrian attributes but omits the parked vehicle present in the ground truth, reducing completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: Pedestrian attributes (action, context, communicative, transporting, age) are well captured, though track_id mismatch and missing vehicle semantics lower the score.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relations like near right curb align with the scene description and in front of ego is plausible, but no spatial info for the missing vehicle and slight uncertainty about ego relation reduces confidence.
justifications.overall: Good pedestrian-level extraction with rich semantics but omission of the parked vehicle and minor tracking inconsistencies limit overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected both people and the parked vehicle but used incorrect track IDs and misclassified the second person's context and transporting status, reducing completeness.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attributes and spatial descriptors beyond the ground truth, increasing richness, but includes an incorrect transporting label and some unsupported fields.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes detailed spatial relations for pedestrians and the vehicle that are plausible, though identity mismatches and one incorrect relation reduce reliability.
justifications.overall: Moderately strong attribute coverage and spatial detail are offset by wrong track IDs and a notable mislabeling of the second person's action/context, lowering overall accuracy.

justifications.completeness: The pedestrian is largely described but the tracker ID differs from ground truth and a parked vehicle present in the scene is entirely omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides detailed pedestrian attributes and multiple contextual relations but omits vehicle information and vehicle state present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Pedestrian spatial relations are well captured including relative direction and nearby landmark, though no spatial information is given for the parked vehicle.
justifications.overall: Good description of the pedestrian and context but the missing vehicle and mismatched track ID reduce the overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The VLM detected only a small fraction of the ground-truth persons (roughly under 25%) and also introduced some person IDs that do not appear in the ground truth, so recall is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many attribute fields (actions, context, communicative, age, spatial relations) for each detected person, but several attribute assignments are incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth (e.g., communicative labels and a standing vs walking error).
justifications.spatial_relations: The VLM supplies detailed spatial relations (sidewalk, crosswalk, left/right locations) but misassigns some key relations (it marks pedestrians on the crosswalk that the ground truth indicates are different IDs and misses others), reducing spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is moderately informative in structure and attributes but suffers from low detection completeness and several important semantic and spatial errors, yielding limited reliability for safety-critical use.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only a small subset of pedestrians (with duplicate track_id entries) and a single vehicle that does not match ground-truth IDs, so much of the scene is missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes various attribute fields (age, actions, transporting, communicative) but overgeneralizes ages to adult, omits many ground-truth communicative labels, and misses numerous person instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Many useful spatial relations are provided (on crosswalk, sidewalks, relative to vehicle) but there are ID mismatches, duplicates, and some incorrect relative placements that reduce reliability.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers some useful semantic and spatial information but is incomplete, contains ID and attribute errors, and misses many ground-truth entities, yielding low overall fidelity.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only a subset of pedestrians and fails to report the ground-truth vehicles, missing many annotated persons and vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides many semantic attributes per pedestrian (actions, contexts, communicative signals, age) showing good richness despite missing entities and some mislabels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Extraction lists numerous spatial relations but contains mismatches with ground truth such as a nonmatching vehicle id and several likely mislocated pedestrians, reducing reliability.
justifications.overall: While semantically detailed for the reported objects, the extraction is incomplete and has notable spatial and object mismatches, limiting overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected 9 of 34 ground-truth pedestrians and reported a different vehicle id, missing the majority of annotated actors.

justifications.spatial_relations: Includes numerous spatial relations, but several appear incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth (vehicle/pedestrian id swaps and wrong crosswalk assignments).
justifications.overall: Some useful semantic detail is present but major omissions, ID mismatches, and incorrect spatial information significantly reduce overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only 8 pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth contains many more persons and two different vehicle tracks, so the detection coverage is very low.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some local spatial relations are plausibly reported (sidewalk, near curb, next to), but many relations are incomplete, a vehicle reference is inconsistent with ground truth, and relation coverage across agents is limited.
justifications.overall: While the extraction is semantically detailed for a few detected agents, it misses most ground truth actors, contains identification and attribute errors, and thus delivers limited overall utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only a small subset of the many annotated pedestrians and misses the true moving vehicle while introducing false track IDs, so coverage is poor.

justifications.overall: While semantically detailed, the extraction has low completeness and several incorrect or missing detections, yielding a below‑acceptable overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction lists only five pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth contains many more person tracks and different vehicle tracks, indicating major omissions and ID mismatches.

justifications.spatial_relations: Many spatial relations are provided, but several refer to incorrect object IDs or a vehicle that does not match the ground truth, reducing spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: The extraction is detailed in attribute coverage but suffers from substantial missing objects, ID and label errors, and incorrect spatial references, yielding limited overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only four pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth contains 28 pedestrians and two vehicles, with only one clear ID match (pedestrian 5) and one vehicle plausibly matching, so most objects are missing or mismatched.

justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for each detected entity and the vehicle is described as ahead of the crosswalk, but many relative references involve nonexistent track ids and some placements contradict the ground truth roster and contexts.
justifications.overall: While the extraction is semantically detailed, its low recall of ground truth entities and multiple ID and attribute mismatches lead to a low overall evaluation.

justifications.completeness: Most pedestrians and all vehicles from the ground truth are missing and several extracted track ids do not match the annotated ids.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only three pedestrians (two matching GT ids and one spurious) and a single generic vehicle while missing the majority of annotated persons and vehicles, so coverage is very low.

justifications.overall: While the extraction is semantically detailed for a few objects, major omissions, several incorrect labels, and an inaccurate vehicle entry lead to a low overall quality score.

justifications.completeness: Only three pedestrians and no vehicles are reported while the ground truth contains over twenty persons and one vehicle, so most entities are missing.

justifications.overall: The output captures a small subset of actors with several wrong labels and missing entities, resulting in poor overall fidelity to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only seven pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth contains many more persons and two vehicles, so most objects are missing or unlabeled with concrete track ids.

justifications.overall: The extraction shows reasonable schema coverage and some useful attributes but suffers from poor detection recall, several incorrect attribute assignments, and inconsistent spatial grounding, yielding limited practical utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while the ground truth contains around 22 people and multiple vehicles, so it is highly incomplete.

justifications.overall: Because the system severely under-detects entities despite reasonable labeling of the few detected ones, the overall extraction quality is low.

justifications.completeness: Only three pedestrians are reported while the ground truth contains many more people and one reported pedestrian is actually a vehicle, so coverage is very poor.

justifications.completeness: Identified 3 of about 30 annotated persons and missed the single parked vehicle, so coverage is very low.

justifications.completeness: Only six pedestrians and no vehicles are reported versus roughly 30 persons and 2 vehicles in the ground truth, so coverage is very low.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians out of many (>40) annotated persons and entirely missed the annotated vehicle, so the extraction is extremely incomplete.
